pull.title: net: cap future-MTP headers commitments
pull.body:
### Problem
Headers presync computes `m_max_commitments` from the elapsed time since the chain-start MTP plus `MAX_FUTURE_BLOCK_TIME`.
When the local clock is more than `MAX_FUTURE_BLOCK_TIME` behind the chain-start MTP, that elapsed value is negative, but it is used in arithmetic assigned to the unsigned commitment cap.
This can turn the intended zero bound into a large cap, letting low-work headers presync continue instead of aborting.

### Fix
Keep the elapsed-time value signed and assign an explicit zero commitment cap when the chain-start MTP is still too far in the future for a locally acceptable extension to exist. Abort presync upfront on the first header batch in that case, instead of waiting for the random commitment offset to reach a commitment slot.

The branch is split into three bisect-friendly commits: a regression test pinning the current (broken) behavior, the minimal fix (test assertions flip), and a logging cleanup. The `headers_sync_state` fuzz target now allows the same clock-skew case instead of always choosing a time at or after the chain-start MTP.

The abort log lines in the same code path are raised as well: the new clock-skew abort and the existing non-continuous-headers abort to `LogWarning`, and the `exceeded max commitments` abort to `LogInfo` with the actual `m_max_commitments` value included.

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Code Coverage & Benchmarks For details see: https://corecheck.dev/bitcoin/bitcoin/pulls/35208. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | Concept ACK | [hodlinator](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35208#pullrequestreview-4266350977) | | Stale ACK | [dergoegge](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35208#pullrequestreview-4251572956), [optout21](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35208#pullrequestreview-4264655473) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore. ### Conflicts Reviewers, this pull request conflicts with the following ones: * [#35114](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35114) (test: NodeClockContext follow-ups by seduless) If you consider this pull request important, please also help to review the conflicting pull requests. Ideally, start with the one that should be merged first.

ACK e201bdb74b994ebb34999a38612401167b02c4a9

Good catch of a boundary condition issue, fixed correctly.

Verified build and the test passing locally.
Reproduced the failure of the new test with the pre-change production code.
Note that the `auto`->`int64_t` change is not strictly needed, but it is correct, and it's safer to be explicit (the compiler resolves `auto` to signed `int64_t` (at least with `c++`/linux)).

Ran the test with these variations:
| variant | type | conditional present | test result |
|---|---|---|---|
| pre-change | auto | no | fail |
|| uint64_t | no | fail |
|| int64_t | no | fail |
|| auto | yes | pass |
|| uint64_t | yes | fail |
| post-change | int64_t | yes | pass |

Concept ACK e201bdb74b994ebb34999a38612401167b02c4a9

Good to tighten up the code!

tree 9e73fdfd4b9bf5a852a2c0b9f01ad23bbcc78575

author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1778533248 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1778536072 +0200

test: cover future chain-start MTP boundary in headers presync

When the local clock sits more than `MAX_FUTURE_BLOCK_TIME` behind the chain-start MTP, the elapsed value used to derive `m_max_commitments` is negative but converted to a huge unsigned bound by the division by the unsigned commitment period.
Presync then accepts the peer's first header and requests more, where it should abort.

Pin that pre-fix behavior in a regression test that the subsequent fix will flip.

events.message:
test: cover future chain-start MTP boundary in headers presync

When the local clock sits more than `MAX_FUTURE_BLOCK_TIME` behind the chain-start MTP, the elapsed value used to derive `m_max_commitments` is negative but converted to a huge unsigned bound by the division by the unsigned commitment period.
Presync then accepts the peer's first header and requests more, where it should abort.

Pin that pre-fix behavior in a regression test that the subsequent fix will flip.

parent 428ff2322d9132880c986de59e2531d7a1f02443
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1778533294 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1778536262 +0200

net: cap future-MTP headers commitments at zero

Keep the elapsed value signed and assign an explicit zero commitment cap when the chain-start MTP is still too far in the future, so `m_max_commitments` no longer wraps to a huge bound.

Flip the regression test to expect presync to abort, and broaden the `headers_sync_state` fuzz target so it covers the same clock-skew case.

Co-authored-by: MarcoFalke <*~=`'#}+{/-|&$^_@721217.xyz>

verification.payload:
tree b020c41608ffd3b17c31935ec0cbd2d93c53654b
parent 421b3defbaf754e951d1bf74d6297981b8d03839
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1778536133 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1778536272 +0200

net: surface headers presync abort reasons in default logs

Add a `LogWarning` for the local-clock-behind-chain-start-MTP abort introduced in the previous commit so the silent early return becomes observable, and raise the existing peer-supplied non-continuous chain abort to `LogWarning` as well.

Raise the `exceeded max commitments` abort to `LogInfo` and include the actual `m_max_commitments` value so the cap is visible in the log line.

Co-authored-by: Hodlinator <172445034+hodlinator@users.noreply.github.com>

comments.body: nit: I think instead of a branch, this can be a plain `ConsumeTime` call with just the `min` adjusted to go further back. This makes the fuzz format a bit more static, and the fuzz engine should still be able to figure out the edge case. You may also extend the input space by allowing time further back than one `MAX_FUTURE_BLOCK_TIME`?

Thanks for the review! I liked the logging ideas, they fit the existing method better than exceptions (which would also need a unique_ptr + try/catch in the fuzz target).

Split into three commits following #35260: test pinning current behavior, minimal fix (cap at zero + upfront abort, test assertions flip), and a final commit unifying the logs (new `LogWarning` for
the clock-skew abort, non-continuous-headers raised to `LogWarning`, and exceeded max commitments to `LogInfo` with `m_max_commitments` included).
